# Fuel Level Reporting

> Read fuel sensor data transmitted by GPS hardware, detect significant fuel drops (theft or fast consumption) and unexpected increases (refuelling), and provide fuel consumption summaries across trips and time periods.

**Category:** Data · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** gps · tracking · fuel · fleet · sensor · alert · consumption

## What this does

Read fuel sensor data transmitted by GPS hardware, detect significant fuel drops (theft or fast consumption) and unexpected increases (refuelling), and provide fuel consumption summaries across trips and time periods.

Specifies 3 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **fuel_level** *(number, optional)*
- **fuel_volume** *(number, optional)*
- **fuel_used** *(number, optional)*
- **fuel_consumption_rate** *(number, optional)*
- **fuel_drop_threshold** *(number, optional)*
- **fuel_increase_threshold** *(number, optional)*

## What must be true

- **0:** Fuel data is read from position attributes; the specific attribute key and unit depend on the device and protocol
- **1:** A fuel drop event is generated when the fuel level decreases by more than fuel_drop_threshold between consecutive positions
- **2:** A fuel increase event is generated when the fuel level increases by more than fuel_increase_threshold between consecutive positions (indicating a refuel)
- **3:** Only latest positions are evaluated for threshold crossings; outdated positions are skipped
- **4:** Fuel consumption is calculated per trip and per summary period using the difference between start and end fuel values
- **5:** If the device does not report a fuel sensor, the feature is inactive and no fuel events are generated
- **6:** Both percentage-based and volume-based fuel attributes are supported; the platform uses whichever the device provides

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **No Fuel Sensor** — when position does not include any fuel attribute, then Fuel events skipped for this position; no fuel data is available from this device.
- **Fuel Drop Detected** — when current fuel value is lower than previous fuel value by >= fuel_drop_threshold; position is the latest for the device, then Fuel drop event stored; possible theft or leak investigation can be triggered.
- **Fuel Increase Detected** — when current fuel value is higher than previous fuel value by >= fuel_increase_threshold; position is the latest for the device, then Fuel increase event stored; refuelling noted for cost tracking.

## Errors it can return

- `FUEL_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND` — The device referenced does not exist

## Connects to

- **gps-position-ingestion**
- **trip-detection**
- **fleet-scheduled-reports**
